text: Welcome to the other side of Wimbledon, the poshest endurance test on the earth.
Here it's all about the friends you make and the champagne you drink while standing in line for hours and hours.
We are at the front of the Wimbledon queue with Florence who has been camping now for two days.
She's come all the way from Hong Kong for this.
Florence, how have your last two nights been?
It has been great.
I met so many friends, new friends and we chat all the way about tennis, about Djokovic, about the park.
Two nights of camping.
You're not a massive camping fan but show us what you're holding.
A special painting.
Yeah, a special painting of Djokovic.
I hope he can win 25 this year.
But some didn't even bring tents.
This is all we brought.
A blanket.
I mean, full credit.
We've never been here before.
Because we only had a blanket and we were freezing, we decided we'll go to the toilets.
We had the airplane pillows, and so we took those in our backpacks and left our blankets.
We didn't care about those and went to the toilet.
And you just can lay your head on the sink.
On the pillow and sit on the toilet.
And we slept for about three hours.
Just with Karim who has been given his Centre Court ticket and gosh he worked hard for this.
Good morning to you.
Tell us you ditched your hotel and tell us what you did for two nights let me give you that.
Yeah so we don't have any tent for two nights I slept with a friend and we only had like two small mattresses and yeah no hotel just like big jacket and that's how we went on for two nights.
But the veterans were more prepared.
You are camping in style, you've got your toothbrushes and wine next to each other on a little table.
What are the tricks to do this?
Well we will not go down on equipment.
No, we will just you know make ourselves as comfortable as possible.
Young and old, people had traveled from all over to be here.
We're the Ricketts family from Danville, California and this is Dorothy Ricketts,
Charlotte Ricketts, DJ Ricketts, Annabel Ricketts, Daisy Ricketts and Rochelle Ricketts.
Wow and tell me why you're here?
We're here to experience Wimbledon, it's the tradition, cue.
We have come from Australia, 52 hour journey to be here today and we're really excited to catch some tennis so yeah we're keen to support the Aussies.

Well it very much feels like the word for today is queuing because this queue behind me that goes all the way round and all the way around here isn't to get into Wimbledon it's for the lose and just to say when you get inside I'm afraid there are no showers
no showers means fresh off a plane from Taiwan these fans have had to get get creative.
It's one of the few sporting events in the world where fans can still turn up on the day and get tickets.
We are playing ball together and it's the first time we are playing ball.
It's his first time, right?
First time playing ball at Wimbledon.
So how do people pass the time?
How long have you been in the queue this morning?
God, like what time is it now?
Is it 8 .30?
So like three, four hours I think.
And show us what you've achieved in that time.
I've only just started this.
I am better than this, I swear.
And would you believe it?
Many of these fans will do it all again tomorrow.
